# Test: apx external transition command
#
# This scenario verifies:
# 1. Transitioning external → forked changes origin and import_mode
# 2. Reverse transition forked → external restores import_mode
# 3. Transitioning a non-external API fails
# 4. Transitioning to current state fails (already at target)

# Setup: Create a canonical repo and register external APIs
mkdir canonical
cd canonical
exec git init
exec apx init canonical --org=testorg --repo=apis --skip-git --non-interactive

# Register an external API
exec apx external register proto/google/pubsub/v1 --managed-repo github.com/Infoblox-CTO/apis-contrib-google --managed-path google/pubsub/v1 --upstream-repo github.com/googleapis/googleapis --upstream-path google/pubsub/v1 --lifecycle stable
stdout 'Registered external API'

# Verify initial state
exec apx external list
stdout 'external'
stdout 'preserve'

# Transition external → forked
exec apx external transition proto/google/pubsub/v1 --to forked
stdout 'Transitioned'
stdout 'forked'

# Verify state changed
exec apx external list
stdout 'forked'
stdout 'rewrite'

# Verify apx.yaml updated
exec cat apx.yaml
stdout 'origin: forked'
stdout 'import_mode: rewrite'

# Reverse transition forked → external
exec apx external transition proto/google/pubsub/v1 --to external
stdout 'Transitioned'
stdout 'external'

# Verify state restored
exec apx external list
stdout 'external'
stdout 'preserve'

# Error: transition to current state (already external)
! exec apx external transition proto/google/pubsub/v1 --to external
stderr 'already'

# Error: transition a non-existent API
! exec apx external transition proto/payments/ledger/v1 --to forked
stderr 'not found'
